how to save electricity at home?
answers: Electricity is the flow of electrons through a conductor. It powers our homes, devices, and almost everything in modern life. It's measured in volts, amps, and watts.
Electricity holo electron er flow. Voltage holo pressure, current holo flow rate, ar resistance holo barrier. Ohm's law diye era relate kora hoy: V = IR.
AC (Alternating Current) changes direction periodically and is used in homes. DC (Direct Current) flows in one direction and is used in batteries and electronics.
To save electricity, use LED bulbs, turn off unused appliances, use energy-efficient devices, and unplug chargers when not in use. Small habits can cut your bill significantly.
